Title: Low-oxygen response is triggered by an ATPdependent shift in oleoyl-CoA in Arabidopsis
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: acts as a signaling metabolite that triggers low-oxygen response via RAP2.12 release
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Acyl-CoA-binding protein family; class II ACBP with ankyrin repeats
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Binds oleoyl-CoA and sequesters RAP2.12 at the plasma membrane under normoxia; oleoyl-CoA promotes dissociation of the ACBP1:RAP2.12 complex, enabling hypoxia gene activation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: acbp1 knockout phenotyping under anoxia; GFP localization; in vitro binding assay with acyl-CoAs; RAP2.12 relocalization assay in vivo
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACBP1 is required for proper low-oxygen tolerance in Arabidopsis. 2. ACBP1 remains at the plasma membrane during hypoxia while RAP2.12 dissociates. 3. C18:1-CoA, but not C16:0-CoA or C18:0-CoA, weakens ACBP1:RAP2.12 interaction and triggers RAP2.12 nuclear accumulation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: hypoxia; anoxia; submergence; exogenous C18:1-CoA/C16:0-CoA/C18:0-CoA treatment
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used for RAP2.12-GFP localization assays; ACBP1 native context otherwise
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a membrane docking/sequestration partner rather than enzyme or transcription factor; central signaling hub integrating acyl-CoA status with RAP2.12 behavior.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune hypoxia signaling, but constitutive disturbance of the ACBP1-RAP2.12 module may misactivate stress responses and reduce fitness or tolerance.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: fatty acid activation and acyl-CoA formation; lipid trafficking between plastid and ER
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: export/activation of plastid-derived long-chain fatty acids for eukaryotic lipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: long-chain fatty acids including palmitic acid (C16:0) and oleic acid (C18:1); ATP; CoA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates long-chain fatty acids by ligating CoA in an ATP-dependent reaction to form long-chain acyl-CoAs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step under hypoxia because activity becomes ATP-limited and shifts the C18:1-CoA/C16:0-CoA ratio
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool and determines signaling acyl-CoA composition that controls hypoxia response
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: signaling trigger for release of RAP2.12 and activation of low-oxygen gene expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced LACS4 activity contributes to elevated C18:1-CoA and reduced C16:0-CoA, causing constitutive induction of hypoxia-responsive genes and impaired low-oxygen tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AMP-binding acyl-activating enzyme family; long-chain acyl-CoA synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: root and shoot tissues as described for LACS at outer plastid membrane
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: double knockout mutant analysis; HPLC acyl-CoA profiling; qPCR of hypoxia genes; in vitro enzyme assay for LACS4 ATP dependence; stress tolerance phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. lacs4 lacs9 double mutants show increased C18:1-CoA and decreased C16:0-CoA in air. 2. These mutants constitutively express RAP2.12-dependent hypoxia genes under aerobic conditions. 3. LACS4 activity is ATP-dependent, linking cellular energy status to acyl-CoA composition and hypoxia signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: hypoxia; anoxia; submergence; ATP limitation; antimycin-A treatment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive perturbation of LACS4/LACS9 lowers tolerance to anoxia and submergence and alters growth/performance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper focuses on LACS activity as the upstream biochemical cause of acyl-CoA pool shift rather than direct measurement of LACS4 transcript regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid constitutive loss of LACS4 activity; if engineered, modulation should be conditional/tissue-specific to prevent maladaptive constitutive hypoxia signaling.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: fatty acid activation and acyl-CoA formation; lipid trafficking between plastid and ER
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: export/activation of plastid-derived long-chain fatty acids for eukaryotic lipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: long-chain fatty acids including palmitic acid (C16:0) and oleic acid (C18:1); ATP; CoA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates long-chain fatty acids by ligating CoA in an ATP-dependent reaction to form long-chain acyl-CoAs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step under hypoxia because loss with LACS4 shifts signaling acyl-CoA composition
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool and determines signaling acyl-CoA composition that controls hypoxia response
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: signaling trigger for release of RAP2.12 and activation of low-oxygen gene expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss together with LACS4 elevates C18:1-CoA/C16:0-CoA ratio, activates hypoxia-response genes in air, and decreases anoxia/submergence tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AMP-binding acyl-activating enzyme family; long-chain acyl-CoA synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: root and shoot tissues as described for LACS at outer plastid membrane
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: double knockout mutant analysis; HPLC acyl-CoA profiling; qPCR of hypoxia genes; stress tolerance phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LACS9 functions redundantly with LACS4 in controlling plastid fatty acid activation/export. 2. Combined loss of LACS4 and LACS9 shifts the endogenous acyl-CoA pool toward C18:1-CoA. 3. This shift is sufficient to induce low-oxygen marker genes under normoxia and compromises stress tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive perturbation of LACS4/LACS9 lowers tolerance to anoxia and submergence and alters plant performance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function inferred from double mutant phenotypes and prior LACS assignment; ATP dependence was directly assayed for LACS4 in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not a simple positive engineering target; constitutive reduction is detrimental because it misregulates hypoxia signaling.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: acts as upstream signaling metabolite for low-oxygen response activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: C18:1-CoA-dependent release from ACBP1 and oxygen-dependent stabilization allow RAP2.12 to accumulate in nuclei and induce hypoxia-responsive genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential condition-dependent activator linking acyl-CoA status and oxygen sensing to hypoxia gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; oxygen-dependent protein stabilization via N-end rule determines output
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: hypoxia-responsive marker genes; RAP2.12-dependent hypoxia-response genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: C18:1-CoA increase; hypoxia/low oxygen; cellular ATP decline; N-end rule oxygen sensing
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms ACBP1:RAP2.12 complex at plasma membrane under normoxia
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane under normoxia; nucleus upon release under hypoxia or C18:1-CoA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: constitutively expressed; accumulates in nucleus under oxygen below 10% or after C18:1-CoA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 35S:RAP2.12-GFP localization assay; in vitro binding assay; qPCR of target genes; genetic evidence from prior and current mutant/overexpression analyses
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RAP2.12 is released from ACBP1 specifically by C18:1-CoA, not by C16:0-CoA or C18:0-CoA. 2. Released RAP2.12 relocalizes to the nucleus and induces hypoxia-responsive genes. 3. Full signaling output additionally requires oxygen-dependent stabilization through the N-end rule pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: hypoxia; exogenous C18:1-CoA; antimycin-A; normoxia versus low oxygen
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive activation of RAP2.12 signaling can reduce hypoxia/flooding tolerance depending on conditions and can impair fitness
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study identifies oleoyl-CoA-triggered release as the missing initial step upstream of oxygen-dependent ERFVII stabilization.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for tuning flooding responses, but constitutive stabilization/activation is risky; conditional control is preferable.
